parsed_review.summary: CP00-328 is a well-formed, internally consistent descriptor-only pack covering the RP10.P04 closeout tail and RP10.P05 permission-foundation head (150 units = 75 P04 + 75 P05). Pack identity, risk class C, unit count, range, and source ledger SHA agree across baseline, manifest, registry binding, closeout-pack-plan.json, and the intake contract. All 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js; the test suite and validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs load fixtures from the authoritative plan/contract (not self-referential), deep-equal the contract projection against freshly-built descriptors, assert coverage/foundation validity, descriptor-only rows, no-leak guards (permission/audit/fixture/cross-tenant/security-trimming/AI/Hermes), the no-write attestation, the authority boundary, handoff routing to CP00-329/RP10.P05.M05.S14, and CP321-327 regression preservation. No side-effecting or runtime patterns (writeFile/fetch/exec/db/network/process.env) exist in the changed source; freeze functions pin every runtime/write/leak flag false. The authority boundary is honored everywhere: Claude is read-only and not the final approver, human final approval is required, and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt shaping (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), never product runtime. The manifest correctly holds production_ready=false / status=in_progress / pack_level_claude_review=pending_review with empty receipts, and the readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON (no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed, fenced, or tool-call-shaped evidence). Downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are not yet present, which is the expected state for this read_only_claude_run step of the hardening sequence; final closeout still requires those artifacts plus human approval by design. One non-blocking P3 documentation gap: the descriptor advertises a README anchor (packages/intake/README.md#cp00-328) that does not exist, as the README only documents CP00-321 through CP00-326. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; pack closeout is not blocked.

findings.title: Descriptor references a non-existent README anchor (#cp00-328)
findings.details: The CP328 descriptor sets documentation_entry='packages/intake/README.md#cp00-328' (service.js:1177, also in INTAKE_CORE_CP328_ROW_EXTRAS at service.js:1111 and mirrored in contracts/intake-core-contract.json), and the descriptor sets index_export_check/documentation references implying a per-pack README section. However, packages/intake/README.md only contains sections for CP00-321 through CP00-326; there is no '## CP00-327' or '## CP00-328' heading, so the advertised anchor does not resolve. This is a pre-existing documentation drift that began with CP327 (README last updated for CP326). Impact is limited to documentation completeness: it does not affect any runtime/security/data boundary, and it does not break validation or tests because neither the validator nor the test suite asserts that the README physically contains the anchor (they only check descriptor/contract string consistency, which is internally consistent). The README intro paragraph already states the descriptor-only boundary applies to every pack in the package, so the documentation is not misleading about boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Add '## CP00-327' and '## CP00-328' sections to packages/intake/README.md (matching the existing per-pack summary style) so the advertised #cp00-328 anchor resolves; alternatively, drop the per-pack fragment from documentation_entry until the README is updated. Non-blocking for CP00-328 closeout.
